targets.expr:
topk(200,
  sum by (region, network, vendor, finality, category) (
    increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      user=~"${user:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
      vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
      category=~"${category:regex}",
      finality=~"${finality:regex}"
    }[$__range])
  )
)

topk(20,
  sum by (vendor) (
    increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
      vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
      category=~"${category:regex}",
      finality=~"${finality:regex}",
      user=~"${user:regex}",
    }[$__range])
  )
)

topk(20,
  sum by (upstream) (
    increase(erpc_upstream_request_total{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
      vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
      category=~"${category:regex}",
      finality=~"${finality:regex}",
      user=~"${user:regex}"
    }[$__range])
  )
)

topk(2,
  sum by (connector) (
    increase(erpc_cache_get_success_hit_total{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
      vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
      category=~"${category:regex}",
      policy=~".*finality=(${finality:pipe}).*",
      user=~"${user:regex}",
    }[$__range])
  )
)

topk(20,
  sum by (region, network) (
    increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
      vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
      category=~"${category:regex}",
      finality=~"${finality:regex}",
      user=~"${user:regex}"
    }[$__range])
  )
)

topk(20,
  sum by (category) (
    increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
      vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
      category=~"${category:regex}",
      finality=~"${finality:regex}",
      user=~"${user:regex}"
    }[$__range])
  )
)

topk(20,
  sum by (finality, vendor_group, category) (
    label_replace(
      label_replace(
        label_replace(
          increase(
            erpc_network_successful_request_total{
              cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
              region=~"${region:regex}",
              network=~"${network:regex}",
              network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
              project=~"${project:regex}",
              vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
              vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
              category=~"${category:regex}",
              finality=~"${finality:regex}",
              user=~"${user:regex}"
            }[$__range]
          ),
          "vendor_group", "vendors", "vendor", ".*"
        ),
        "vendor_group", "CACHE", "vendor", "<cache>"
      ),
      "vendor_group", "CACHE", "vendor", "prism"
    )
  )
)

panels.description: Total successful responses sent back to the client

targets.expr:
sum(increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",user=~"${user:regex}"
    }[1m])) by (region, agent_name, user) > 0

panels.title: Total Responses (by Region and User-Agent)

panels.description: Total successful responses sent back to the client

targets.expr:
sum(increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",user=~"${user:regex}"
    }[1m])) by (region) > 0

panels.title: Total Responses (by Region)

panels.description: Total requests received by eRPC for a specific network and method

targets.expr:
sum(increase(erpc_network_request_received_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regexp}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}",
    namespace=~"${namespace:regex}"
}[1m])) by (region, namespace, network, category, finality)

panels.title: Incoming Requests

panels.description: Total requests received by eRPC for a specific network and method

targets.expr:
sum(rate(erpc_network_request_received_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    network!~"${ignore_network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}",
    namespace=~"${namespace:regex}",
}[$__rate_interval])) by (region, namespace, network, user, category, finality) > 0

panels.title: Incoming RPS

panels.description: Total successful responses sent back to the client

targets.expr:
sum(rate(erpc_network_successful_request_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    vendor!~"${ignore_vendor:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}",
    namespace=~"${namespace:regex}",
}[1m])) by (region, namespace, network, vendor, category, finality, emptyish) > 0

panels.title: Successful Responses RPS

panels.description: When a request fails even after trying all upstreams and retries, we send a error response to the user. This chart shows critical errors that need attention and wake-up alarms.

targets.expr:
sum(increase(erpc_network_failed_request_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    severity="critical",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}",
    namespace=~"${namespace:regex}"
}[1m])) by (network,namespace, category, finality,error) > 0

panels.title: Network-level Critical Errors

panels.description: Issues where clients got an error but it's most probably due to bad requests or a transient problem with upstreams

targets.expr:
sum(increase(erpc_network_failed_request_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    severity="warning",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}"
}[1m])) by (network,category,error,region, user) > 0

panels.title: Network-level Warnings

panels.description: Client-side errors where it's most probably a normal operation (eth_call reverts) or bad request by the user

targets.expr:
sum(increase(erpc_network_failed_request_total{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    severity="info",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}"
}[1m])) by (network,category,error,user) > 0

panels.title: Network-level Notices

targets.expr:
histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_bucket{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
    vendor!="<error>",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}"
}[1m])) by (le, network, category))

histogram_quantile(0.9, sum(rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_bucket{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
    vendor!="<error>",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}"
}[1m])) by (le, network, category, finality))

histogram_quantile(0.5, sum(rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_bucket{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
    vendor!="<error>",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}"
}[1m])) by (le, network, category, finality))

histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_bucket{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    vendor="<error>",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}"
}[1m])) by (le, network, category, finality, vendor))

histogram_quantile(0.9, sum(rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_bucket{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    vendor="<error>",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}"
}[1m])) by (le, network, category, finality, vendor))

histogram_quantile(0.5, sum(rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_bucket{
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    vendor="<error>",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}"
}[1m])) by (le, network, category, finality, vendor))

panels.description: Dynamic timeout duration computed per request from method latency percentiles. Shows how the quantile-based timeout adapts to real traffic patterns.

targets.expr: rate(erpc_network_timeout_duration_seconds_sum{network=~"${network:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[5m]) / rate(erpc_network_timeout_duration_seconds_count{network=~"${network:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[5m])
histogram_quantile(0.99, rate(erpc_network_timeout_duration_seconds_bucket{network=~"${network:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[5m]))

panels.title: Dynamic Timeout Duration

panels.description: Current calculated quantile threshold used to trigger hedged requests. This depends on the hedge.quantile and actual latencies (e.g. p70)

targets.expr: avg(erpc_network_hedge_delay_seconds{network=~"${network:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}) by (project, network, category, finality)

panels.title: Hedge Quantile

panels.description: Total hedge requests fired due to initial upstream being slow

targets.expr: sum(rate(erpc_network_hedged_request_total{network=~"${network:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, category, finality)

panels.title: Hedge RPS

panels.description: Total hedged requests that ended up being actually faster and useful. The higher the better. If this value is too low try increasing the failsafe.hedge.quantile to higher value e.g. 0.99

targets.expr: 1 - ((sum(increase(erpc_network_hedge_discards_total{attempt!="1",network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[30s])) by (project, network)) / (sum(increase(erpc_network_hedged_request_total{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network))) > 0

panels.title: Hedge Effectiveness

panels.description: When identical requests arrive at the same time they'll be merged, this number shows number of these requests

targets.expr: sum(rate(erpc_network_multiplexed_request_total{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, category, finality)

panels.title: Multiplexed RPS

panels.description: Shows the finality state of the block related to the response, this shows if your workload is mainly around head of chain unfinalized data, or historical finalized data.

targets.expr: sum(increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{network=~"${network:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regexp}",upstream=~"${upstream:regexp}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (finality)

panels.title: Response Finality Share Overall

panels.description: Shows the finality state of the block related to the response, this shows if your workload is mainly around head of chain unfinalized data, or historical finalized data.

targets.expr: sum(increase(erpc_network_successful_request_total{network=~"${network:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regexp}",upstream=~"${upstream:regexp}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (vendor, finality)

panels.title: Response Finality Share By Vendor

panels.description: Highest value for latest block polled from all upstreams across all replicas

targets.expr: max(erpc_upstream_finalized_block_number{upstream="*",network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}"}) by (project, network)

panels.title: Highest Finalized Block

panels.description: Highest value for latest block polled from all upstreams across all replicas

targets.expr: max(erpc_upstream_latest_block_number{upstream="*",network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}"}) by (project, network)

panels.title: Highest Latest Block

panels.description: Comparison of block timestamp distance between EVM State Poller and Network Responses to identify cache staleness and synchronization issues

targets.expr: avg(erpc_network_latest_block_timestamp_distance_seconds{network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",origin="evm_state_poller"}) by (network)

panels.title: Block Timestamp Distance - EVM State Poller

panels.description: Comparison of block timestamp distance between EVM State Poller and Network Responses to identify cache staleness and synchronization issues

targets.expr: avg(erpc_network_latest_block_timestamp_distance_seconds{network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",origin="network_response"}) by (network)

panels.title: Block Timestamp Distance - Network Response

panels.description: EMA-estimated block time per network, dynamically computed from local block detection timestamps

targets.expr: max by (network, project) (erpc_network_dynamic_block_time_milliseconds{network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"})

panels.title: Block Time

panels.description: EMA-estimated block time per network, dynamically computed from local block detection timestamps

targets.expr: max by (network, project) (erpc_network_dynamic_block_time_milliseconds{network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}) / 1000
1 / max by (network, upstream) (rate(erpc_upstream_latest_block_polled_total{network!="",network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Block Time

targets.expr: (1 / max by (network, upstream) (rate(erpc_upstream_latest_block_polled_total{network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval]))) / on(network) group_left() (max by (network) (erpc_network_dynamic_block_time_milliseconds{network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}) / 1000)
max by (network, upstream) (erpc_upstream_block_head_lag{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"})

panels.description: Catch-up retries/sec by reason — ONLY the data-not-yet-available reasons (block_unavailable/empty_result/missing_data) that take the block-time wait. Failover retries (retryable_error/pending_tx) are in the separate 'Failover Retries' panel so they don't mask catch-up.

targets.expr: sum by (network, reason) (rate(erpc_network_retry_attempt_total{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",reason=~"block_unavailable|empty_result|missing_data"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Catch-up Retries by Reason

panels.description: p95 of the catch-up wait per reason. Reliable once the dedicated catch-up buckets ship (network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds); expect p95 ≈ one block time per chain. If it looks pinned high, cross-check erpc_network_dynamic_block_time_milliseconds and the avg (rate(_sum)/rate(_count)) before blaming the EMA — coarse buckets used to make this misleading.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, network, reason) (rate(erpc_network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_bucket{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])))

sum by (network, reason) (rate(erpc_network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_sum{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum by (network, reason) (rate(erpc_network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_count{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Catch-up Wait — p95 per Wait by Reason

panels.description: How many requests are stuck in a catch-up wait AT ONCE, per network/reason. Computed as rate(network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_sum) = wait-seconds accrued per second; by Little's law that equals the average number of requests simultaneously blocked in a catch-up wait. Example: 4 = about 4 requests are always sitting in a catch-up sleep. This is the concurrent latency load catch-up imposes. Flat/low (<1) = negligible; a sustained climb = more requests stuck waiting (upstream-freshness problem). Note slow chains weigh more — one 12s mainnet wait counts the same as twelve 1s waits — so compare a network against its own baseline, not across chains.

targets.expr: sum by (network, reason) (rate(erpc_network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_sum{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Catch-up Wait Pressure (≈ concurrent requests waiting)

targets.expr:
(sum by (network, upstream) (count_over_time((erpc_selection_position{cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",network=~"^${network:regex}$",method="*"} == 0)[10m:60s])) * 1000 - (avg by (network, upstream) (avg_over_time(erpc_selection_score{cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",network=~"^${network:regex}$",method="*"}[10m])) or (sum by (network, upstream) (count_over_time((erpc_selection_position{cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",network=~"^${network:regex}$",method="*"} == 0)[10m:60s])) * 0)))
== on(network) group_left()
max by (network) ((sum by (network, upstream) (count_over_time((erpc_selection_position{cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",network=~"^${network:regex}$",method="*"} == 0)[10m:60s])) * 1000 - (avg by (network, upstream) (avg_over_time(erpc_selection_score{cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",network=~"^${network:regex}$",method="*"}[10m])) or (sum by (network, upstream) (count_over_time((erpc_selection_position{cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",network=~"^${network:regex}$",method="*"} == 0)[10m:60s])) * 0))))

panels.title: Active Primary Upstream (per network)
panels.description: Fleet-majority PRIMARY upstream per network over time, smoothed over a 10m trailing window so brief replica churn collapses to the dominant upstream (one continuous block) while real fail-overs still show. Across replicas the most-routed upstream (position==0) wins; ties break toward the better selection_score. Public RPC vendors get fixed colors; others use the per-network palette; segment borders separate adjacent blocks. Paginates when networks exceed the panel height.

Network-scope FAILOVER retries/sec (retryable_error/pending_tx) — genuine upstream errors / tx hunting, NOT chain catch-up. A spike here means upstreams are erroring; drill into 'Upstream Errors by Type'.

targets.expr: sum by (network, reason) (rate(erpc_network_retry_attempt_total{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$",reason=~"retryable_error|pending_tx"}[$__rate_interval]))

panels.title: Failover Retries by Reason

panels.description: Top upstream error sources by error CODE (ErrEndpointUnsupported, ErrEndpointCapacityExceeded, ...). The 'why' behind failover retries — e.g. an upstream sent methods it doesn't support, or rate-limited.

targets.expr: topk(15, sum by (network, upstream, error) (rate(erpc_upstream_request_errors_total{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: Upstream Errors by Type

panels.description: Share of each network's requests that hit a catch-up wait, by reason. 'How many requests are affected' — e.g. a chain at 15% means ~1 in 7 requests waits for not-yet-available data.

targets.expr: 100 * (sum by (network, reason) (rate(erpc_network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_count{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])) / on(network) group_left() sum by (network) (rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_count{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: Catch-up — Requests Affected (% of network requests)

panels.description: Catch-up wait time spread across ALL of a network's requests = the average latency tax catch-up adds per request. The bottom-line negative effect. (Per-AFFECTED-request overhead is in 'Catch-up Wait'.)

targets.expr: 1000 * (sum by (network) (rate(erpc_network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_sum{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])) / sum by (network) (rate(erpc_network_request_duration_seconds_count{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: Catch-up — Amortized Overhead (ms / request)

panels.description: Catch-up retries split by request finality. unfinalized/realtime = normal tip races. FINALIZED should be ~0: finalized data is permanent, so waiting a block won't make it appear — catch-up there means genuinely-missing data / an incomplete archive upstream (fail over, don't wait). 'unknown' = finality couldn't be classified; treat like finalized for triage.

targets.expr: topk(20, sum by (network, finality) (rate(erpc_network_data_unavailable_wait_seconds_count{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}[$__rate_interval])))

panels.title: Catch-up by Finality (finalized ≈ red flag)

targets.expr:
sum by (vendor) (
  erpc_network_successful_request_total{
    region=~"${region:regex}",
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    network=~"${network:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",
    vendor!="n/a",
    category=~"${category:regex}",
    finality=~"${finality:regex}"

)

sum by (vendor, finality) (

    region=~"${region:regex}",

    network=~"${network:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",
    user=~"${user:regex}",
    vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",

    category=~"${category:regex}",

)

panels.title: Upstream stale lower-bound
Eligible Upstreams per Network
panels.description: Number of upstreams currently in rotation per network. Drops mean the policy excluded one; if it hits 0, the safety net (`whenEmpty`) is serving the raw set.

targets.expr:
histogram_quantile(0.50, sum by (le) (rate(erpc_selection_eval_duration_seconds_bucket{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}"
  }[5m])))

histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le) (rate(erpc_selection_eval_duration_seconds_bucket{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}"
  }[5m])))

histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le) (rate(erpc_selection_eval_duration_seconds_bucket{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}"
  }[5m])))

panels.title: Eval Duration p50/p95/p99
panels.description: Per-tick JS eval cost. Should sit well under `selectionPolicy.evalTimeout` (default 100ms). Spikes correlate with sobek GC or large upstream pools.

targets.expr:
sum by (kind) (rate(erpc_selection_eval_errors_total{
      cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
      region=~"${region:regex}",
      project=~"${project:regex}",
      network=~"${network:regex}",
      network!~"${ignore_network:regex}"
  }[5m]))

panels.title: Eval Errors by Kind
panels.description: kind: `timeout` (eval ran past evalTimeout), `throw` (JS runtime exception), `invalid_return` (eval returned non-array), `fallback_default` (engine fell back to the canonical default). Healthy = flat zero.

panels.title: Score per Upstream
panels.description: `sortByScore` output, HIGHER = better. Score = overall(u) / (1 + Σ metric×weight). Per-upstream `routing.scoreMultipliers.overall` boosts visibly lift the line.

panels.title: Position per Upstream
panels.description: Current rank in the order. 0 = primary, 1+ = backup, -1 = excluded. Averaged across instances; a value like 0.4 means the upstream is primary on 40% of instances.

panels.title: Currently Stuck-Excluded (>60s)
panels.description: Upstreams continuously excluded for >60s. Alert on >600s for 'stuck out >10 min'. Filter by $upstream/$network to drill in.

panels.title: Exclusion Rate by Reason
panels.description: `excludeIf` trips per second, per leaf-predicate slug (`error_rate_above`, `latency_p95_above`, `block_head_lag_above`, ...). Option-(c) attribution: compound predicates emit one increment per leaf.

targets.expr: topk(15, max by (network, upstream) (erpc_selection_excluded_seconds{network=~"^${network:regex}$",project=~"^${project:regex}$",cluster=~"^${cluster:regex}$"}))

panels.title: Top Excluded (current duration)
panels.description: Top-15 upstreams by how long they are CURRENTLY continuously excluded (erpc_selection_excluded_seconds, wall-clock). 0 = in rotation. This is duration, not event count — pair with the 'Active Primary Upstream' timeline to see the exclusion windows.

panels.title: Exclusions by Upstream & Reason
panels.description:
Per-upstream exclusion rate, by leaf-reason (top-20 series). Tells you exactly WHICH upstream is being dropped for WHICH rule (`error_rate_above`, `latency_p70_deviation_above`, `block_head_lag_above`, ...). Drives root-cause: an upstream spiking on `error_rate_above` is a different problem from one spiking on `throttle_rate_above`.

**Reading the y-axis**: exclusion events per second across all eRPC instances (sum of per-instance × per-tick increments where this upstream tripped this rule). With N instances × 1Hz eval interval, a sustained N events/sec means every instance excludes this upstream every tick (always tripping); 1 event/sec means roughly 1 in N instances excludes per tick (intermittent / borderline).

panels.title: Primary Switches per Network
panels.description: Rate of primary-upstream changes per network. Sustained high rate = the policy is flapping (close-scoring contenders). Consider tightening `stickyPrimary.hysteresis` for that network.

panels.title: Sticky Holds per Upstream
panels.description: Ticks where `stickyPrimary` ACTIVELY held the incumbent against a challenger. High count = sticky is doing real anti-flap work.

panels.title: Readmit Age Distribution
panels.description: How long upstreams were out before being readmitted. Should peak near `readmitExcluded.reAdmitAfter` (90s in the default). Anything in the tail = stuck excluded longer than intended.

panels.title: Shadow Exclusions by Reason (dry-run signal)
panels.description: `shadowExcludeIf` trips — upstreams that WOULD have been excluded if the rule were real. Compare to `selection_exclusion_total` to decide when to flip a shadow rule to a real `excludeIf`.

panels.title: Cordon Events (admin RPC)
panels.description: Manual `erpc_cordonUpstream` / `erpc_uncordonUpstream` admin RPC events. Action: `cordon` / `uncordon`. Spikes = operator intervention.

targets.expr: avg(erpc_upstream_block_head_lag{upstream!="*",network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regex}"}) by (project, network, upstream) <= 5000

panels.description: A special lagging tracker showing only lags of above 5000 blocks which indicates node too far off.

targets.expr: avg(erpc_upstream_block_head_lag{upstream!="*",network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regex}"}) by (project, upstream, network,category) > 5000

panels.title: Out-of-sync latest block lag by Upstream

targets.expr: avg(erpc_upstream_latest_block_number{upstream!="*",network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}"}) by (cluster, project, namespace, region, upstream, network)

panels.description: How many times we had to Poll for latest block number. This number can go up by integrity module if debounce config is too low.

targets.expr: sum(increase(erpc_upstream_latest_block_polled_total{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, upstream)

panels.title: Latest Block Polls

panels.description: Difference between lowest and highest finalized block as reported by upstreams. Min/Max/Mean are values across multiple replicas (if you have erpc in a multi-container setup)

targets.expr: avg(erpc_upstream_finalization_lag{upstream!="*",network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regex}"}) by (project, upstream, network) <= 5000

panels.title: Finalized Block Lag by Upstream

panels.title: Out-of-sync Finalized Blocks by Upstream

targets.expr: avg(erpc_upstream_finalized_block_number{upstream!="*",network!="",network=~"${network:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}"}) by (project, upstream, network)

panels.description: How many times we had to Poll for finalized block number.

targets.expr: sum(increase(erpc_upstream_finalized_block_polled_total{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",vendor=~"${vendor:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, upstream)

panels.description: These are issues that are most often edge-cases that are not covered within eRPC logic, and must be reported to the team.

histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(erpc_cache_set_error_duration_seconds_bucket{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}"}[1m])) by (le, project, network, category, connector, policy, ttl))

panels.description: Block range each read-through cache connector (e.g. gRPC indexer) currently serves, per network (PR #909): earliest / latest / finalized.

targets.expr: max by (network, connector) (erpc_cache_connector_earliest_block_number)
max by (network, connector) (erpc_cache_connector_latest_block_number)
max by (network, connector) (erpc_cache_connector_finalized_block_number)

panels.title: Cache Connector Block Heads

panels.description: Blocks the cache connector holds beyond its finalized head (latest - finalized) per network/connector. A cross-network "behind chain tip" join is not possible here because cache_connector metrics key network by evm:<chainId> while upstream metrics key by the network alias.

targets.expr: max by (network, connector) (erpc_cache_connector_latest_block_number) - max by (network, connector) (erpc_cache_connector_finalized_block_number)

panels.title: Cache Unfinalized Depth (latest - finalized)

panels.description: Wall-clock age of the latest block each cache connector has ingested (now − its latest block timestamp). Self-contained cache catch-up signal; no join required. Source: cache_connector_latest_block_timestamp_seconds.

targets.expr: time() - max by (network, connector) (erpc_cache_connector_latest_block_timestamp_seconds)

panels.title: Cache Latest-Block Age (staleness)

targets.expr: sum(increase(erpc_consensus_total{cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (network, category)

histogram_quantile(1, sum(rate(erpc_consensus_responses_collected_bucket{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (le, network, category, short_circuited, finality))

    region=~"${region:regex}",
    cluster=~"${cluster:regex}",
    project=~"${project:regex}",

}[1m])) by (network, upstream, category, finality, response_type, larger_than_consensus) > 0
sum(increase(erpc_consensus_short_circuit_total{network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (network, category, reason, finality) > 0
sum(increase(erpc_consensus_errors_total{network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (network, category, category, error, finality) > 0
sum(increase(erpc_consensus_cancellations_total{network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (network, category, phase, finality) > 0

panels.description: Dynamically tuned value for maxCount of different rate limit budgets. NOTE This value is SUM of all replicas (in a multiple container setup)

targets.expr: avg by (budget, method) (erpc_rate_limiter_budget_max_count{})

panels.title: Rate-limit budget MaxCount

targets.expr: sum(rate(erpc_rate_limits_total{origin="project",project=~"${project:regex}",network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, budget)

sum(rate(erpc_rate_limits_total{origin="network",project=~"${project:regex}",network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}",budget!="<remote>"}[1m])) by (project, network, user)

sum(rate(erpc_rate_limits_total{origin="upstream",budget="<remote>",project=~"${project:regex}",network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, vendor)

sum(rate(erpc_rate_limits_total{origin="upstream",budget!="<remote>",project=~"${project:regex}",network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, vendor)

sum(rate(erpc_rate_limits_total{origin="auth",project=~"${project:regex}",network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, user, auth)

sum(rate(erpc_rate_limits_total{network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",user=~"${user:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, category, origin, budget)

sum(rate(erpc_rate_limiter_failopen_total{network=~"${network:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",category=~"${category:regex}"}[1m])) by (project, network, category, origin, budget)

histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(erpc_network_evm_get_logs_range_requested_bucket{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (le, project, network, user, finality))

histogram_quantile(0.5, sum(rate(erpc_network_evm_get_logs_range_requested_bucket{network=~"${network:regex}",upstream=~"${upstream:regex}",project=~"${project:regex}",finality=~"${finality:regex}"}[1m])) by (le, project, network, user, finality))
